description: Plugin-distributed hooks for ravenclaude-core. Auto-merged with the consumer's session hooks when the plugin is installed. Paths use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} so they resolve to the plugin's install location, not the consumer's project root.

hooks.comment: Auto-format files after agent edits. No-ops silently if no formatter is configured for the file type.

Advisory: warns when an edit to a plugin agent definition file looks like the agent is instructing itself to spawn another sub-agent — only the Team Lead dispatches. Set RC_GUARD_RECURSIVE_SPAWN_STRICT=1 to make this blocking.

Advisory (Claim Grounding & Source Honesty): nudges when an unhedged absolute capability claim ('you can't…', 'not possible') is written into a knowledge/ or docs/ markdown file without an inline provenance marker — a durable false prior the next session would trust. Opt-in (no-op unless the project has a .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml); skips frontmatter/code-fences/blockquotes/headings + conditional phrasings; honors a `claim-lint-ok` line escape. Exit 0 always; never blocks. Cannot see the chat answer (no hook can) — this is the durable-artifact surface only.
Block obviously destructive commands that slipped past the consumer's deny list. Reads the tool call as JSON on stdin; exits 2 to block (exit 1 would not block).

Command review (the Thing) — T3 panel tribunal. No-ops (fast grep) unless a category has `thing: on` in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. When a command's category is toggled on, convenes up to three reviewer seats in parallel (Forseti/Mímir/Heimdall) + Thor on a split, renders ALLOW/DENY/EDIT, writes a Sága-log audit entry, emits hookSpecificOutput (with updatedInput on EDIT). The internal panel deadline (~75s) stays under this 90s hook timeout so the script emits its own verdict before the platform's fail-OPEN-on-timeout fires. Fails closed (deny for high-stakes categories, ask for shell_readonly) on its own errors. Live for shell_readonly, shell_remote_mutate, shell_code_exec, shell_local_mutate, shell_package_install.

Runaway / rabbit-hole brake. Portable replacement for native auto-mode's 3/20 block brake (unavailable under Copilot CLI / multi-model). No-ops unless .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml exists; counts tool calls per session and exits 2 (block) when the agent thrashes (default 8 byte-identical calls in a row) or blows a generous total-call ceiling (default 1200). Tune under `runaway:` or disable with `runaway: off`. Deterministic, no model.
Layout enforcement. Reads .repo-layout.json from the consumer's project root; no-ops silently if the manifest is absent.

Decision-review enforcement (the Thing, decision mode). No-ops (fast grep) unless `decision_review: advisory|binding` is set in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml (default off). When opted in AND the AskUserQuestion is a single, binary, non-multiselect yes/no-shaped question, routes it through thing-decide.py; on a BINDING yes/no verdict it DENIES the prompt with the verdict + reasoning so the agent proceeds without interrupting the user. Defer / advisory / high-blast / low-confidence / any error -> allow (the human answers). Every routed decision is Saga-logged under .ravenclaude/runs/thing/decisions/.

Definition-of-done gate. No-ops unless `definition_of_done.cmd` is set in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. When set AND source changed this session, runs the gate command (tests/build/lint) at Stop and BLOCKS the stop (decision:block) until it passes — closing the 'looks done != is done' hallucination gap. Self-limited to max_blocks (default 8) consecutive blocks so it can't deadlock. Deterministic; ports to Copilot via the adapter's stop mode.

If any source files changed this session, remind the Team Lead to run the full test suite before reporting done. Advisory fallback when no definition_of_done.cmd is configured (dod-gate.sh no-ops in that case).

Regenerates the comfort-posture project-layer rules from .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml at session start so the dashboard-composed posture is active without a manual /set-posture. No posture file -> silent no-op. Always exits 0; never blocks. This is the load-bearing wiring for the web flow, where only the committed project layer survives a fresh container.

Advisory: warns when a session loads in acceptEdits/bypassPermissions mode, which bypasses the comfort-posture allow/ask/deny rules. The real pin is permissions.defaultMode=default (written by /set-posture). Reads permission_mode from stdin; never blocks.

Injects a session-start capability banner via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext so the agent opens every session aware of the project's external surface, its detected auth (env-var NAMES/presence only — never values), and its effective .claude/settings.json permissions. Cheap + local (no network, no CLI subprocesses); points to the environment-discovery skill for deep enumeration. Fail-silent; never blocks (SessionStart hooks cannot). Salience boost for the Capability Grounding Protocol, not enforcement.
